description: Four positions (TCOM, CIHKY, IDCBY, PDD) were entered on China recovery or growth narratives simultaneously, creating dangerous correlated exposure to a single country's macro, regulatory, and geopolitical risk. All four resulted in losses within 10 days as China risk-off sentiment swept the entire ADR space regardless of individual company fundamentals. The losses were amplified by thin OTC liquidity in CIHKY and IDCBY, making exits costly.
recommendation: Implement a hard rule capping total China-domiciled or China-revenue-dependent exposure at 15% of any portfolio at time of entry, and ban OTC-listed Chinese ADRs from Core portfolio classifications entirely.

pattern: Narrative Momentum Without Near-Term Catalyst Anchor
description: TCOM, NET, CRM, CACI, and PDD were all entered on directionally plausible theses (travel recovery, security momentum, AI integration, defense spending, Temu growth) but none had a confirmed binary catalyst — such as an earnings release, contract award, or government stimulus announcement — scheduled within the 10-day holding window. The thesis-to-timeframe mismatch caused all five to lose money as broader market sentiment overrode the narrative. Multi-quarter growth stories executed as short-swing trades is a structural setup failure.
recommendation: Before any swing trade entry, require a documented near-term catalyst (earnings date, product launch, contract announcement, or macro event) occurring within 7 days of entry; if no such catalyst exists, the position is disqualified from the swing portfolio regardless of thesis quality.

pattern: Early Exit Discipline Preserving Capital on Large-Cap Tech
description: META (4-day hold, -2.2%) and GOOGL (3-day hold, -2.58%) both demonstrated that rapid recognition of unfavorable price action and discretionary early exits limited losses to under 3%, dramatically outperforming the 6-19% losses absorbed on full 10-day holds in other positions. This pattern proves the trader has the instinct to cut losers quickly but applies it inconsistently, allowing other positions like ASGN (-19%) and TCOM (-16%) to run to full loss. The inconsistency suggests stop-loss rules exist behaviorally but are not systematized.
recommendation: Codify the early-exit behavior observed in META and GOOGL into a universal hard stop-loss rule set at 3-4% below entry price for all swing positions at the moment of purchase, removing discretion and ensuring the discipline applied to large-cap tech is applied uniformly across all holdings.

pattern: Domestic Event-Driven Positions Outperform Macro Recovery Bets
description: The three strongest Core performers — WSM (+6.33%), EIX (+3.82%), and PNC (+4.43%) — all shared a common structure: a domestic company with a specific, identifiable fundamental driver (retail brand momentum, a filed rate case, and a confirmed Fed rate pause respectively). By contrast, the losing Core positions relied on macro recovery narratives in foreign markets or cyclical sector sentiment. The winning pattern required no geopolitical assumption and had a dated, verifiable catalyst, which compressed uncertainty and improved timing precision.
recommendation: Prioritize Core position construction around domestic companies with a single identifiable, dated catalyst (rate case filing, earnings release, product cycle) over macro recovery narratives in foreign markets; require every Core entry to have a named catalyst with a date attached before capital is deployed.

pattern: High-Multiple Growth Stocks Entered Without Technical Breakout Confirmation
description: NET and CRM were both entered on momentum or narrative without a confirmed technical breakout level or defined support structure, leaving the positions fully exposed to multiple compression during any risk-off tape rotation. High-revenue-multiple growth stocks carry asymmetric downside sensitivity to rate expectations and macro sentiment shifts, meaning entry timing precision is more critical than for value or cyclical names. Both positions lost roughly 6-7%, which is significant for a 10-day swing horizon and indicates the entry was made into resistance rather than off support.
recommendation: For any stock trading above 15x revenue, require a confirmed weekly chart breakout above a prior resistance level with above-average volume as a non-negotiable entry criterion, and reduce standard position sizing by 25% to account for elevated multiple-compression risk in risk-off environments.
